data.content:
Root cause
- All-repositories routing appears correct: multi-repo rows keep `_repoPath`, and the beat detail flow passes `detailRepo` through `fetchBeat` and `updateBeatOrThrow`.
- The corruption is in `src/lib/backends/knots-backend.ts`: both `create()` and `update()` serialize `input.acceptance` via `knots.updateKnot(..., { addNote: "Acceptance Criteria:\n..." })`.
- `toBeat()` only stringifies raw knot notes into `beat.notes`; it never extracts acceptance back into `beat.acceptance` or strips acceptance-marker notes. That explains the repro and the strange block already visible in knot data (for example `foolery-d984` currently carries an `Acceptance Criteria:` note entry in `kno ls --json`).
- Existing coverage in `src/lib/__tests__/knots-backend-coverage.test.ts` currently locks the write-side behavior but not the read-side round-trip, so the regression is effectively unguarded.

Implementation plan
1. Add acceptance-note helpers in `src/lib/backends/knots-backend.ts` that detect notes beginning with the exact `Acceptance Criteria:` marker, derive `beat.acceptance` from the latest such note, and exclude those tagged notes from visible `beat.notes`.
2. Wire those helpers into `toBeat()` so knots beats round-trip acceptance separately from notes in every view, including all-repositories mode and detail refetches.
3. Keep write-path compatibility in `create()` and `update()` by centralizing the acceptance marker string/helper instead of duplicating inline note writes. Treat a blank marker body as an explicit clear so acceptance can be unset cleanly.
4. Update `src/lib/__tests__/knots-backend-coverage.test.ts` to cover the real contract:
   - knots acceptance round-trips into `beat.acceptance`
   - visible `beat.notes` excludes acceptance-marker notes
   - repeated acceptance edits use the latest tagged note as source of truth
5. If implementation uncovers ambiguity in mixed-repo presentation, add a narrow guardrail assertion in `src/lib/__tests__/knots-guardrails.test.ts` so all-repositories mode keeps `_repoPath` annotation while still surfacing acceptance separately.

Validation strategy
- Run focused Vitest coverage for the knots backend regression first.
- Run `bun run lint`.
- Run `bunx tsc --noEmit`.
- Run broader tests only if the focused backend changes touch shared behavior.

Complexity and risks
- Estimated complexity: low to medium. The likely fix is contained to the knots backend plus tests.
- Main constraint: knots currently exposes append-only note writes, not a first-class acceptance field. The fix therefore needs an emulation layer that treats the newest tagged acceptance note as source of truth while hiding tagged entries from the visible notes field.
- Raw knot history will still retain older acceptance-note revisions; this is acceptable for now so long as the Foolery beat contract stays stable and the UI no longer shows those entries as ordinary notes.
- No child knots look necessary at planning time unless implementation reveals a separate cleanup/migration track.
